text.title: w3c-design-tokens-standard Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for w3c-design-tokens-standard as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent emits unresolved alias references (e.g., `{color.brand.primary}`) in the final output file without verifying the referenced token exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broken aliases cause Style Dictionary builds to fail or silently produce empty values; validate the resolved token map before commit.

failure-mode.description: Agent applies DTCG format to a single-app project with fewer than 50 tokens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DTCG format overhead (schema, tooling, resolver) is not justified for small, single-platform projects; CSS custom properties in one file are sufficient.

failure-mode.description: Agent normalizes an existing Tokens Studio file to DTCG without running `token-organization` first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Format migration does not fix structural problems (raw-value naming, no semantic layer); applying DTCG to a poorly organized set produces a well-formatted mess.
